title: Leftover Lamb Bobotie
excerpt: Meat layer 250 ml milk 2 large eggs 2 thick slices white bread, broken into cubes 30 ml sunflower oil 1 large, finely chopped onion 45 ml sugar 45 ml curry powder 10 ml turmeric 30 ml white vinegar 4 x 250 ml leftover lamb, cut into 1 cm pieces or flaked 250 ml …
html:
Ingredients

Meat layer

  • 250 ml milk
  • 2 large eggs
  • 2 thick slices white bread, broken into cubes
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 1 large, finely chopped onion
  • 45 ml sugar
  • 45 ml curry powder
  • 10 ml turmeric
  • 30 ml white vinegar
  • 4 x 250 ml leftover lamb, cut into 1 cm pieces or flaked
  • 250 ml seedless raisins
  • 125 ml fruit chutney
  • salt and white pepper to taste
  • a handful of flaked almonds
  • 6 lemon or bay leaves

Custard topping

  • 3 large eggs
  • 375 ml milk
  • salt and pepper to taste

Method:

For the meat layer: Whisk the milk and eggs together and pour over the bread cubes.

Mix well and set aside to soak.

Heat the oil in a pan and fry the onion until glassy, then add the sugar and curry powder and fry for another minute or so to cook the raw taste out of the curry.

Stir in the turmeric and vinegar and add the mixture to the meat.

Add the raisins and chutney, season to taste and mix lightly.

If needed, break any large pieces of bread up with a fork and mix well into the meat mixture.

Spoon the mixture into an ovenproof dish (or small ovenproof dishes) and bake at 190°C for 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, whisk the topping ingredients together, take the meat out of the oven and pour the custard over the meat.

Sprinkle the bobotie(s) with flaked almonds and tuck the lemon or bay leaves into the custard.

Now bake further at 180°C for 20 – 30 minutes or until the topping has set.

Serve 6 portions with rice, chutney and a tomato-vegetable sambal (*).

Easy!

(*) Tomato-vegetable sambal: Mix small cubes of tomato, onion, cucumber and green or red bell pepper. Season to taste with finely chopped green chilli, finely chopped coriander leaves, plain vinegar, salt, white pepper and sugar to taste. Delicious!
